panels.description: Medium shot: Inside the humble Cratchit home, Bob Cratchit sits at a wooden table, looking hopeful as he holds a small calling card. Peter, a young boy in a worn waistcoat, leans in with interest. The room is dimly lit by a flickering hearth.

bubbles.text: Very well observed, my boy! If I can be of service to you in any way,' he said, giving me his card, 'that's where I live. Pray come to me.'

panels.description: Close-up shot: Bob Cratchit’s face, his eyes glistening with unshed tears and a small, sad smile of gratitude. The lighting is soft and warm, highlighting his weathered but kind features.

bubbles.text: It really seemed as if he had known our Tiny Tim, and felt with us.

panels.description: Medium shot: Mrs. Cratchit, wearing a patched apron, places a hand on Peter's shoulder while looking at Bob. Peter looks slightly embarrassed but hopeful. They are gathered in the small, cramped kitchen.

bubbles.text: You would be surer of it, my dear, if you saw and spoke to him.
I shouldn't be at all surprised if he got Peter a better situation!

panels.description: Wide shot: The entire Cratchit family huddled together in an emotional embrace. Bob stands in the center, looking solemn. The lighting is low and melancholic, casting long shadows against the modest walls.

bubbles.text: However and whenever we part from one another, I am sure we shall never forget poor Tiny Tim.
Never, father! I am very happy, I am very happy!

panels.description: Wide shot: A drastic shift in tone. Scrooge and the Spirit of Christmas Yet To Come stand in a dark, foggy, and desolate London court. The Spirit is a tall, hooded figure in deep black robes, silent and imposing. Scrooge points toward a nearby building with a trembling hand.
characters: Spirit of Christmas Yet To Come

bubbles.text: Spectre, this court... is where my place of occupation is. Let me behold what I shall be, in days to come!

panels.description: Medium shot: The Spirit has stopped at an iron gate. Its long, skeletal finger points directly at a gravestone in the distance. Scrooge stares at the hand, his face etched with mounting dread and horror.
characters: Spirit of Christmas Yet To Come

bubbles.text: The Spirit stopped. The inexorable finger underwent no change.

panels.description: Close-up shot: Scrooge’s face, pale and glistening with sweat, looking up toward the Spirit's hidden face. His eyes are wide with desperation, his mouth partially open as he pleads.

bubbles.text: Before I draw nearer to that stone to which you point, ask me one question.
